title: Create secret with replace option fails if secret does not exist
body:
### Issue Description

When creating a secret the command fails if replace option is used and the secret does not exist

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Check if secret does not exist

podman secret ls

ID                         NAME               DRIVER      CREATED       UPDATED

2. Create the new secret with replace option

echo '123' | podman secret create --replace test -

### Describe the results you received

Error: deleting secret : : no secret data with ID

### Describe the results you expected

A new secret should be created

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: I dont think this is a bug? The command is doing precisely as it should.  It also seems to follow the manpage to the letter; that said, it seems like a reasonable feature request and i have labeled it as such.  perhaps you would be interested in submitting a pull request for this?

I think the manuals are a bit misleading here. Nowhere is explicitely stated what happens if the secret does not exist. But for the following reasons I think it is a bug or at least not the intended behaviour:

1. Other commands with the --replace option run sucessfully and do create a new item if an item with that name does not exist:

podman pod create --replace test
podman kube play --replace test.yml
podman create --name=test --replace docker.io/library/alpine:latest

2. The error message looks odd compared to the command to remove a secret:

echo '123' | podman secret create --replace test -
Error: deleting secret : : no secret data with ID

podman secret rm test
Error: no secret with name or id "test": no such secret

3. Imho a replace option makes no sense if it only replaces an item. The main use case is to create an item in any case, if it exists or not. So we can run single commands without removing an item with an extra command beforehand.
Please open a PR for this, I agree secret create --replace should work like the other --replace objects.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
